projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

insights.summary: The network consistently shows 10-12 connected devices, jumping to 12 at 19:11, which exceeds the typical count for the four household members and strongly suggests guests or friends are visiting this Friday evening. This normal social pattern is worth noting for occupancy context and system adjustments.

The rapid oscillation between 12 and 13 connected devices over the last two hours, with the total count remaining consistently high, indicates active device movement and packing typical of a morning routine, suggesting the family is still preparing to depart rather than having already left for school or work.

Roxanne's Eight Sleep tracker reports 17.71 hours of sleep, which is physically impossible for a single night and likely indicates a sensor glitch or data sync error. Cross-referencing with stable presence data confirms no unusual activity, making this a low-priority device anomaly.

The presence count consistently shows 11-14 mobile devices, significantly exceeding the household size, and fluctuates frequently without any corresponding camera activity, suggesting either unregistered guests on the network or a device intermittently connecting and disconnecting.

The rapid drop in connected devices from 20 to 13 between 09:07 and 09:13 strongly indicates a coordinated household departure, likely the children heading to school and adults leaving for work. This confirms the home is transitioning to an unoccupied state, making it the ideal trigger for security and energy-saving automations.

The presence count consistently shows 17-20 devices home from 06:29 to 07:59 AM without any camera activity during typical morning prep hours, which may indicate overnight guests, IoT device tracking artifacts, or simply a need to verify connected devices against the household size.

The persistent 16-18 device count at 3 AM paired with zero camera, motion, or audio triggers indicates background network pinging or IoT devices rather than human occupancy, which helps prevent false occupancy or intrusion alerts.

The persistent 16-18 device count at 3 AM, combined with zero camera activity or other sensor triggers, strongly suggests network double-counting or IoT devices being misclassified as mobile phones rather than actual human presence. This is likely a benign reporting artifact, but worth monitoring to rule out unauthorized network connections.

The network consistently reports 16-18 mobile devices present at 2 AM, which is unusually high for a four-person household and suggests either unexpected guests are staying over or the presence system is incorrectly tracking IoT devices. This warrants a quick check to ensure no unauthorized access or network misconfiguration.

The network consistently reports 15-19 connected devices, far exceeding the household's expected count, which combined with no camera activity at 9:57 PM strongly suggests an unlogged gathering, visitors, or a large number of active IoT devices. This warrants a quick check to ensure network stability and confirm who is on the premises.
